cases.passage: The local authorities in the city of Noida have launched a massive campaign to promote e-waste recycling. The city generates a large amount of electronic waste every year, which includes old computers, mobile phones, televisions, and refrigerators. The authorities have set up several e-waste collection centers across the city where citizens can drop off their old electronic devices. The collected e-waste is then sent to a recycling facility where it is processed and converted into raw materials such as copper, gold, and silver. The authorities are also providing awareness programs to educate citizens about the importance of e-waste recycling and the harmful effects of dumping e-waste in landfills.

opts: (A) To reduce the amount of waste sent to landfills
questions.explanation: E-waste recycling is important because it reduces the amount of waste sent to landfills, conserves natural resources, and reduces greenhouse gas emissions.

questions.q: What happens to the collected e-waste in Noida?
opts: (A) It is sent to a landfill
(B) It is processed and converted into raw materials
(D) It is exported to other countries
questions.explanation: The collected e-waste in Noida is processed and converted into raw materials such as copper, gold, and silver.

questions.q: What is the role of the local authorities in Noida?
opts: (B) They set up e-waste collection centers
(C) They provide awareness programs to citizens
questions.explanation: The local authorities in Noida are responsible for setting up e-waste collection centers, providing awareness programs to citizens, and collecting e-waste from citizens.

questions.q: What is the impact of e-waste on the environment?
opts: (C) It pollutes the soil and water
questions.explanation: E-waste can pollute the soil and water if not disposed of properly, which can have severe environmental consequences.

questions.q: What is the benefit of e-waste recycling?
opts: (A) It reduces the amount of waste sent to landfills
questions.explanation: E-waste recycling has several benefits, including reducing the amount of waste sent to landfills, conserving natural resources, and generating revenue.

cases.passage: A local school in Delhi has launched a project to reduce e-waste generated by its students. The school has implemented a program where students can donate their old electronic devices to a local charity, which then sells them to raise funds for the school. The school has also set up a recycling center where students can drop off their old batteries, cords, and other electronic waste. The students are also educated about the importance of e-waste recycling and the impact of e-waste on the environment.

questions.q: What is the goal of the school's e-waste reduction project?
opts: (B) To reduce e-waste generated by students
questions.explanation: The goal of the school's e-waste reduction project is to reduce the e-waste generated by its students.

questions.q: What is the role of the students in the school's e-waste reduction project?
opts: (A) They collect e-waste from their peers
(B) They educate their peers about e-waste recycling
(C) They drop off their old electronic devices at the recycling center
questions.explanation: The students play a crucial role in the school's e-waste reduction project, including collecting e-waste from their peers, educating their peers about e-waste recycling, and dropping off their old electronic devices at the recycling center.

questions.q: What is the impact of e-waste on the environment?
opts: (C) It pollutes the soil and water
questions.explanation: E-waste can pollute the soil and water if not disposed of properly, which can have severe environmental consequences.

questions.q: What is the benefit of the school's e-waste recycling program?
opts: (A) It reduces the amount of waste sent to landfills
(C) It generates revenue for the school
questions.explanation: The school's e-waste recycling program has several benefits, including reducing the amount of waste sent to landfills, conserving natural resources, and generating revenue for the school.

questions.q: What is the outcome of the school's e-waste reduction project?
opts: (B) The school reduces e-waste generated by students
questions.explanation: The outcome of the school's e-waste reduction project is that the school generates revenue, reduces e-waste generated by students, and promotes e-waste recycling.
